description.en: John's ridiculous joke surprised Mina. Learn various reactions to confirm if what someone said is actually true.
description.ko: 존의 황당한 농담에 미나가 깜짝 놀랐어요. 상대방의 말이 사실인지 확인하는 다양한 리액션을 배워봅니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up guys. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분. Real Reaction 코스에 오신 걸 환영합니다. 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone. Welcome to the Real Reaction course. I'm Mina.

insight.title: 안녕하세요 여러분
insight.description: A formal greeting to a group
insight.usage_tip: Perfect for starting a podcast or a speech.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I have some big news. I decided to quit this show and become a professional mukbang YouTuber.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: What.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진짜야?

insight.title: 진짜야?
insight.description: The most common way to ask 'Is it real?' in casual speech
insight.usage_tip: Used when you hear something unexpected.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Yes. I'm going to eat 50 bowls of spicy ramen tomorrow.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말이야?

insight.title: 정말이야?
insight.description: Very similar to 'Jinjja-ya' but focuses more on 'Is it the truth'

content.speaker: John
content.text: Hahaha. Just kidding. I could never leave our Talkori listeners.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 에이. 깜짝이야. 진짜인 줄 알았잖아요.
content.translation: Hey. You scared me. I thought it was for real.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. So today we are learning how to ask. Are you serious.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국어로는 진짜야. 그리고 정말이야. 이 두 표현을 가장 많이 써요.
content.translation: That's right. In Korean. We use 'Jinjja-ya' and 'Jeongmal-iya' the most.

content.speaker: John
content.text: They sound very similar. Is there a difference.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진짜 is 'Real'. 정말 is 'True'.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 거의 똑같지만. 진짜야. 이 표현이 훨씬 더 자주 쓰여요.
content.translation: 'Jinjja-ya' is used much more often in daily life.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What about when I want to know if someone is being sincere. Like. For real.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 진심이야. 라고 물어보세요.

insight.title: 진심이야?
insight.description: Asking about someone's sincerity or deep intent
insight.usage_tip: Use this when someone makes a big promise or a shocking decision.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진심이야?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 완벽해요. 그리고 만약에 나쁜 상황이라서. 아니길 바랄 때는. 설마 진짜야? 라고 합니다.
content.translation: Perfect. And if it's a bad situation. And you hope it's not true. Say 'Seol-ma jinjja-ya?'.

insight.title: 설마 진짜야?
insight.usage_tip: Used when you strongly doubt or fear that a negative situation might be true.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Ah. So '설마' adds that 'Don't tell me' feeling.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 마지막으로 상대방이 한 말을 다시 확인하고 싶을 땐. 진짜라고? 라고 끝을 올려보세요.
content.translation: Right. Finally. If you want to double check what they said. Say 'Jinjja-rago?' with a rising tone.

insight.title: 진짜라고?
insight.usage_tip: Used to re-confirm a shocking fact that someone just stated.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's practice with a real situation. I'll be the one with the crazy news again.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 좋아요. 이번엔 속지 않을 거예요.
content.translation: Okay. I won't be fooled this time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. Do you see that cat over there. It just said. Hello. to me.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 뭐라구요?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진짜야?

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm serious. The cat has a deep voice too.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 설마 진짜야?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: John. 지금 저 놀리는 거 아니죠? 정말이야?
content.translation: John. You're not teasing me right? Is it true?

content.speaker: John
content.text: I swear. I am being sincere right now.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진심이야?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Yes. And look. The cat is wearing a tiny hat too.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진짜라고?
content.translation: You are saying it is real?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Gotcha. It's just a YouTube video on my phone.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아. 또 당했네. 진짜인 줄 알았잖아요.
content.translation: Ah. Fooled again. I thought it was for real.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow. Mina is so easy to prank.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: John 씨 연기가 너무 리얼해서 그래요.
content.translation: It is because your acting is so realistic. John.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review the five reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. 진짜야?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. 정말이야?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. 진심이야?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. 설마 진짜야?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. 진짜라고?
content.translation: You are saying it is real?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 친구가 놀라운 이야기를 하면 꼭 되물어 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone. Please try asking back when your friend tells you something surprising.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's it for today. We'll be back with another reaction soon.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 만나요.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕.

vocabulary.example: 제 진심을 알아주세요. (Please know my sincerity.)
방금 한 말은 농담이에요. (What I just said is a joke.)
저를 속이지 마세요. (Please don't trick me.)
연기가 정말 리얼하네요. (The acting is really realistic.)
